content.text:
05-28473-11

Description of the Problem
The problem has a function that takes in an integer and returns its sum. The input is taken from stdin (stdin) so we can use standard input to get it for our purposes. We don't need any special functions, just make sure you know what each value represents before using them! If something goes wrong with your code at this point or if you are unsure about how to handle data types correctly then please let me know here!


What I will do
For my part, I am going to look into some basic math concepts, which includes number theory, arithmetic operations, and basics of programming. I won't be doing much except writing pseudocode based on those basics, but I plan to write a program instead. In general, numbers have multiple meanings, including positive values (even though they may seem odd), negative values (because of their meaning), zero, etc. For example, 6 = +6 because the sum of -6 equals -6.

5-12914-15

Description/Explanation of the Solution
My solution would require adding up all the negatives in 6 plus -6 and dividing by 2. This calculation could potentially take anywhere between two minutes to one hour depending on how fast you type your line. As long as you follow these instructions I think your solution should work. However, there might be some bugs in your implementation, so I recommend checking out the documentation before attempting to replicate this method.
If you come across problems like this, feel free to ask away!
